title: Vivarium Population Spenser: Mortality protocol

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8muvq","text":"The rates used in the Mortality component are found here:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ajhgr","text":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Mortality2011_LEEDS1_2.csv","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":105}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"9610C9BE19E911EBB41B0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://github.com/alan-turing-institute/daedalus/blob/develop/persistent_data/Mortality2011_LEEDS1_2.csv"}}}}
steps: Divide the annual mortality rates for a given local authority by the number of time steps existing in a year.
Select all individuals in the sample that appear as "alive" and have an associated gender.
For these individuals, get the mortality rate given their age, gender, ethnicity and location and turn it into a probability.
Using random sampling and the mortality probability of the individuals, choose which individuals die in that time step. Change their status from "alive" to "dead".
creator: Camila Rangel Smith
